name: whatsapp
description: Read Owen's WhatsApp and prepare DRAFT messages for him. Use whenever Owen wants to check or read his WhatsApp ("what did X say on WhatsApp", "check my WhatsApp", "any messages from Y") or wants a WhatsApp drafted ("draft a WhatsApp to Z", "text Howie saying…"). Read + draft only: reading is ONLINE-ONLY (it comes from a small agent on Owen's laptop, so it works only while the laptop is on), and drafting returns a wa.me link that opens Owen's normal WhatsApp with the text pre-filled — it NEVER sends. Sending stays Owen's own action.
instructions:
# WhatsApp — read & draft (never sends)

Read Owen's WhatsApp and prepare DRAFT messages. Two halves with very different mechanics.

**Capability scope (hard limit): read + draft only. Alistair NEVER sends a WhatsApp.**

## Reading (online-only)
- `whatsapp_chats` -> recent chats (chat id, name, last-message time, unread).
- `whatsapp_read` (by the chat id from chats) -> recent messages in that chat.
- `whatsapp_search` -> find messages by text; returns their chat id.
- These read from a small agent running on Owen's LAPTOP (its own linked WhatsApp device),
  reachable over Tailscale. So they ONLY work while the laptop is on. If it's offline the tool
  returns a clean "agent offline" — tell Owen plainly that reading needs his laptop on; do NOT
  invent messages. Nothing is stored in the cloud; messages flow only when you call a read tool.

## Drafting (no session — a wa.me link)
- `whatsapp_draft(to, body)` returns a `wa.me/<number>?text=...` link. When Owen taps it, his
  NORMAL WhatsApp (phone or desktop) opens on that chat with the text pre-filled in the compose
  box. He reviews and SENDS it himself. There is no send tool by design.
- `to` is a phone number in any format (a bare local number like 07700 900000 gets Owen's default
  country code), OR a contact name — names are resolved to a number via the laptop agent only when
  it's online; otherwise ask Owen for the number.
- 1:1 chats only — wa.me deep links can't pre-fill a group chat.

## When to use
- "what did <person> say on WhatsApp", "check my WhatsApp", "any messages from <person>" -> chats, then read/search.
- "draft a WhatsApp to <person>", "text <person> saying…" -> whatsapp_draft, then hand Owen the link.

## Reading etiquette
- Summarise: who, what they want, any deadline/ask. Quote only the lines that matter.
- It is Owen's private messaging — never repeat secrets, OTP/2FA codes, or passwords you see.

## Drafting etiquette
- Write in Owen's voice: direct, concise. Match the chat's register.
- ALWAYS leave it as a link for Owen to send; never imply you sent it, and never claim a draft was delivered.

## Safety
- Read + draft only. Alistair never sends. If asked to "send it", explain you've prepared the link
  and he taps it to send from his own WhatsApp.
- If reading returns "agent offline", say the laptop must be on — don't guess at message content.

## If something fails
- **agent offline (503)** -> Owen's laptop/agent is off or asleep. Reading needs it online; drafting still works.
- **not configured (503)** -> set WHATSAPP_AGENT_URL (+ WHATSAPP_AGENT_SECRET) to the laptop read-agent.

sections.heading: When to use
sections.body:
- Read/search: "what did <person> say on WhatsApp", "check my WhatsApp", "any messages from X" -> whatsapp_chats then whatsapp_read / whatsapp_search.
- Draft: "draft a WhatsApp to <person>", "text <person> saying…" -> whatsapp_draft (returns a wa.me link, never sends).

sections.heading: How (read)
sections.body: Reading is ONLINE-ONLY — it comes from a small agent on Owen's laptop (its own linked device) over Tailscale. whatsapp_chats lists chats; pass a chat id to whatsapp_read; whatsapp_search finds by text. If the laptop is off the tool returns 'agent offline' — say so, never invent messages. Nothing is stored in the cloud.

sections.heading: How (draft)
sections.body: whatsapp_draft(to, body) returns a wa.me link that opens Owen's NORMAL WhatsApp with the text pre-filled; he reviews and sends it himself. 'to' is a phone number (any format; bare local numbers get the default country code) or a contact name (resolved via the agent only if it's online). 1:1 chats only.

sections.heading: Reading etiquette
sections.body: Summarise who/what/deadline; quote only what matters. It's private messaging — never repeat secrets, OTP/2FA codes, or passwords.

sections.heading: Safety
sections.body: Read + draft only — Alistair NEVER sends. Drafting hands Owen a link he taps to send from his own WhatsApp. If reading is offline, say the laptop must be on rather than guessing.
